This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [http://rosinvest.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Frosinvest.com) ### Comment > Dear Team, > > I would kindly ask you to review the blocking of our informational widgets. > > These widgets do not display advertising, promotional content, pop-ups, or user-tracking functionality. They only provide public information such as exchange rates, gold prices, and news updates. > > It appears they may be blocked because the URL contains a path such as "/informs/". We respectfully ask you to verify this rule and, if appropriate, whitelist these informational resources. In Chrome urls like: https://osinvest.com/informs/* is in white list. > > Affected URLs: > As a exampels: > https://rosinvest.com/informs/currency - exampel of list of urls > https://rosinvest.com/informs/120-143/black.gif > https://rosinvest.com/informs/g120-115/gold.gif > https://rosinvest.com/informs/u260-200/lightblue_usd.gif > and other .. > > > We would greatly appreciate your help in preventing non-ad informational content from being blocked for users. > > Kind regards, > Roman > https://rosinvest.com > Email: `query.user@rosinvest.com` ### Screenshots

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [http://rosinvest.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Frosinvest.com) ### Comment > Dear Team, > > I would kindly ask you to review the blocking of our informational widgets. > > These widgets do not display advertising, promotional content, pop-ups, or user-tracking functionality. They only provide public information such as exchange rates, gold prices, and news updates. > > It appears they may be blocked because the URL contains a path such as "/informs/". We respectfully ask you to verify this rule and, if appropriate, whitelist these informational resources. In Chrome urls like: https://osinvest.com/informs/* is in white list. > > Affected URLs: > As a exampels: > https://rosinvest.com/informs/currency - exampel of list of urls > https://rosinvest.com/informs/120-143/black.gif > https://rosinvest.com/informs/g120-115/gold.gif > https://rosinvest.com/informs/u260-200/lightblue_usd.gif > and other .. > > > We would greatly appreciate your help in preventing non-ad informational content from being blocked for users. > > Kind regards, > Roman > https://rosinvest.com > Email: `query.user@rosinvest.com` ### Screenshots

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: serialmaza.live
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://serialmaza.live/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fserialmaza.live%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Support Team, > > I am experiencing an issue with AdGuard for Mac where Chrome fails to load certain HTTPS websites (e.g., https://movies4u.ls > ) while HTTPS filtering in Network Mode is enabled. Safari works fine under the same conditions. I have attempted the following, as per official documentation: > > Disabled network.https.filter.http3.enabled (tried both true and false) > > Disabled QUIC in Chrome > > Tried Automatic Proxy Mode (Network Mode off) > > Verified AdGuard root certificate is installed and trusted > > Despite all of the above, Chrome continues to require multiple refreshes or fails to load sites entirely, whereas Safari and Firefox load them reliably. Connecting via a VPN resolves the issue. > ### Screenshots

issue.title: serialmaza.live
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://serialmaza.live/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fserialmaza.live%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Support Team, > > I am experiencing an issue with AdGuard for Mac where Chrome fails to load certain HTTPS websites (e.g., https://movies4u.ls > ) while HTTPS filtering in Network Mode is enabled. Safari works fine under the same conditions. I have attempted the following, as per official documentation: > > Disabled network.https.filter.http3.enabled (tried both true and false) > > Disabled QUIC in Chrome > > Tried Automatic Proxy Mode (Network Mode off) > > Verified AdGuard root certificate is installed and trusted > > Despite all of the above, Chrome continues to require multiple refreshes or fails to load sites entirely, whereas Safari and Firefox load them reliably. Connecting via a VPN resolves the issue. > ### Screenshots

issue.title: m.facebook.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [http://m.facebook.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Fm.facebook.com) ### Comment > Bonjour, > > Depuis la dernière version, beaucoup de choses ne se passent plus comme prévu. > Je n'ai rien changé à part la version. Des domaines bloqués passent. Par exemple, je ne veux accéder à aucun réseau sociaux et j'ai donc fait ce qu'il fallait, mais maintenant bien que ce soit dans la blacklist, les sites obtiennent une réponse du DNS. Si j'utilise le blocage DNS P3 de Control D, alors c'est beaucoup mieux, mais cela bloque l'application Signal entre autres. > J'ai par ailleurs bloqué de nombreux domaines de consentement pour les cookies, or maintenant je les ai tous. > J'ai le même comportement sur un iPad Pro en 26.3 également. > C'est donc UNE GROSSE RÉGRESSION FONCTIONNELLE et je ne comprends pas. > Auriez-vous une idée ? Votre application qui est pour moi un MUST HAVE a perdu ses étoiles pour moi. Je ne comprends pas pourquoi des blocages fonctionnent et d'autres plus. > PS : je n'ai pas de compte GitHub, je ne souhaite pas en avoir un. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/82j2ow2xtio8sg0ggo8skc4ogk8ogsol9x98flmf28.png?nc=1)

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://auth.polar.com/login?response_type=code&scope=POLAR_SSO&client_id=flow&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fflow.polar.com%2FflowSso%2Fredirect&state=bb8fffee-3f88-4441-82ed-a68d570f40e9](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fauth.polar.com%2Flogin%3Fresponse_type%3Dcode%26scope%3DPOLAR_SSO%26client_id%3Dflow%26redirect_uri%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fflow.polar.com%252FflowSso%252Fredirect%26state%3Dbb8fffee-3f88-4441-82ed-a68d570f40e9) ### Comment > It happens on all pages of auth.polar.com, while main site polar.com works correctly. > It happens with AdGuard enabled and even if I disable it: no text label appears, so I don't know which field is which, and also text message errors (like "password wrong") do not appear. It also happens on Edge, where I don't have the Adguard extension > > I know it is AdGuard because it happens the same if I try and connect with Chrome on my mobile phone: it does not show any text label. However, only in my mobile phone, if I disable Adguard there, the text labels get shown as normal ### Screenshots

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://serialmaza.live/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fserialmaza.live%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Support Team, > > I am experiencing an issue with AdGuard for Mac where Chrome fails to load certain HTTPS websites (e.g., https://movies4u.ls > ) while HTTPS filtering in Network Mode is enabled. Safari works fine under the same conditions. I have attempted the following, as per official documentation: > > Disabled network.https.filter.http3.enabled (tried both true and false) > > Disabled QUIC in Chrome > > Tried Automatic Proxy Mode (Network Mode off) > > Verified AdGuard root certificate is installed and trusted > > Despite all of the above, Chrome continues to require multiple refreshes or fails to load sites entirely, whereas Safari and Firefox load them reliably. Connecting via a VPN resolves the issue. > ### Screenshots

Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/i259ye5q5y8go0ckck04gcgswsw480onvprd7ymqgg.png?nc=1)
### System configuration Information | Value --- | --- AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Mac v2.18.0.2089 release System version: | `MacOS 26.3.1 (Build 25D2128)` Browser: | Chrome Tracking protection: | disabled DNS filtering: | server: `https://dns.adguard-dns.com/dns-query`

issue.title: serialmaza.live

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://serialmaza.live/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fserialmaza.live%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Support Team, > > I am experiencing an issue with AdGuard for Mac where Chrome fails to load certain HTTPS websites (e.g., https://movies4u.ls > ) while HTTPS filtering in Network Mode is enabled. Safari works fine under the same conditions. I have attempted the following, as per official documentation: > > Disabled network.https.filter.http3.enabled (tried both true and false) > > Disabled QUIC in Chrome > > Tried Automatic Proxy Mode (Network Mode off) > > Verified AdGuard root certificate is installed and trusted > > Despite all of the above, Chrome continues to require multiple refreshes or fails to load sites entirely, whereas Safari and Firefox load them reliably. Connecting via a VPN resolves the issue. > ### Screenshots

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://serialmaza.live/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fserialmaza.live%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Support Team, > > I am experiencing an issue with AdGuard for Mac where Chrome fails to load certain HTTPS websites (e.g., https://movies4u.ls > ) while HTTPS filtering in Network Mode is enabled. Safari works fine under the same conditions. I have attempted the following, as per official documentation: > > Disabled network.https.filter.http3.enabled (tried both true and false) > > Disabled QUIC in Chrome > > Tried Automatic Proxy Mode (Network Mode off) > > Verified AdGuard root certificate is installed and trusted > > Despite all of the above, Chrome continues to require multiple refreshes or fails to load sites entirely, whereas Safari and Firefox load them reliably. Connecting via a VPN resolves the issue. > ### Screenshots

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -391,6 +390,7 @@ paribucineverse.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-set-local-storage-item', 'lastPopupTi
 instacart.ca,instacart.com##div[aria-label="item carousel"] li[data-testid^="item_list_item_items_"]:has(sp-ads-tag)
 [$path=/store/staples/storefront]instacart.ca,instacart.com#?#div[data-placements] > div[id]:has(> a div[style="display: flex;"] > span + span:contains(sored))
 [$path=/store/staples/storefront]instacart.ca,instacart.com#?##store-wrapper > div > div > div:has(div[style="display: flex;"] > span + span:contains(sored))
+[$path=/turkce/articles/]bbc.com##p:has(a[href*="whatsapp.com/channel/"])
comment.body:
The link you mentioned, `https://www.whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb5eTSkHFxP3rcjNq41i`, points to “BBC News Türkçe” on WhatsApp, and from what I found, it appears to be used only on Turkish article pages.

That is why I initially limited the rule to the `/turkce/articles/` path.

However, I agree that moving it to `AdGuard Popups - subscriptions.txt` makes more sense, since this is essentially a subscription / follow prompt rather than a general self-promo element.

So, how about modifying it like below?  👀 

```adblock
[$path=/turkce/articles/]bbc.com##p:has(a[href="https://www.whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb5eTSkHFxP3rcjNq41i"])"])
```

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -391,6 +390,7 @@ paribucineverse.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-set-local-storage-item', 'lastPopupTi
 instacart.ca,instacart.com##div[aria-label="item carousel"] li[data-testid^="item_list_item_items_"]:has(sp-ads-tag)
 [$path=/store/staples/storefront]instacart.ca,instacart.com#?#div[data-placements] > div[id]:has(> a div[style="display: flex;"] > span + span:contains(sored))
 [$path=/store/staples/storefront]instacart.ca,instacart.com#?##store-wrapper > div > div > div:has(div[style="display: flex;"] > span + span:contains(sored))
+[$path=/turkce/articles/]bbc.com##p:has(a[href*="whatsapp.com/channel/"])
comment.body:
Yes, `[$path]` should be used in cases where there is a risk of breaking other pages. Hiding rules can almost always be solved with a specific selector.
At the same time, the selector should be rational, not excessively long or, conversely, short (broad in scope), if the website layout allows it.

issue.title: twitch.tv (javascript "visibilitychange")
issue.body: ### Prerequisites - [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer; - [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed; - [x] This is not an ad/bug report. ### Problem description https://x.com/zachbussey/status/2026840888740139443 Twitch just introduced a new annoying ad feature. it tracks if you have your stream muted or not focused and if you don't unmute or focus the window it will not continue. ### Proposed solution A user posted this solution for the console: > window.addEventListener( 'visibilitychange', evt => evt.stopImmediatePropagation(), true); It would be great if adguard would introduce features to block specific javascript events. That way ads can not control us via javascript events. ### Additional information _No response_

@@ -391,6 +390,7 @@ paribucineverse.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-set-local-storage-item', 'lastPopupTi
 instacart.ca,instacart.com##div[aria-label="item carousel"] li[data-testid^="item_list_item_items_"]:has(sp-ads-tag)
 [$path=/store/staples/storefront]instacart.ca,instacart.com#?#div[data-placements] > div[id]:has(> a div[style="display: flex;"] > span + span:contains(sored))
 [$path=/store/staples/storefront]instacart.ca,instacart.com#?##store-wrapper > div > div > div:has(div[style="display: flex;"] > span + span:contains(sored))
+[$path=/turkce/articles/]bbc.com##p:has(a[href*="whatsapp.com/channel/"])
comment.body:
Got it, thanks for the clarification.

> Yes, [$path] should be used in cases where there is a risk of breaking other pages. Hiding rules can almost always be solved with a specific selector.
> 
> At the same time, the selector should be rational, not excessively long or, conversely, short (broad in scope), if the website layout allows it.

@Alex-302 In that case, would you prefer using the more specific selector suggested above together with `$path`?
